title: tephroseris praticola
intro: Tephroseris praticola, also known as the alpine marsh cinquefoil, is a flowering plant that belongs to the family Asteraceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as prevent the growth of bacteria and fungi. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including its ability to lower blood pressure, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. Tephroseris praticola can be prepared as a tea or tincture, and is often used in herbal remedies to treat a range of health issues, including colds, fever, and digestive problems.
